content:
# Clay Mineralogy

**Summary**: The study of clay minerals, which are hydrous aluminum phyllosilicates that significantly impact reservoir properties.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2015]] investigated the role of different clay types (kaolinite, illite, chlorite) on oil recovery and rock integrity.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] analyzed clay-mineralogy to identify kaolinite, illite, and polygorskite as key components.

- [[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017]] analyzed clay-mineralogy to determine the impact of specific minerals on shale stability.

- [[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021]] used clay content as a color code to distinguish lithofacies and determine the best RPM.

- [[2022-alshalan|Alshalan, 2022]] analyzed the impact of different clay mineral types on shale swelling.

- [[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021]] analyzed the clay mineralogy to understand the impact of smectite on swelling.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] investigated different OCs including montmorillonite, sepiolite, and palygorskite.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2015]] Clay minerals are the most dominant reactive surfaces in sandstone reservoirs and affect oil mobility.

- [[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016]] analyzed clay-mineralogy to identify kaolinite, illite, and polygorskite as key components.

- [[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017]] analyzed clay-mineralogy to determine the impact of specific minerals on shale stability.

- [[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021]] used clay content as a color code to distinguish lithofacies and determine the best RPM.

- [[2022-alshalan|Alshalan, 2022]] analyzed the impact of different clay mineral types on shale swelling.

- [[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021]] analyzed the clay mineralogy to understand the impact of smectite on swelling.

- [[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024]] investigated different OCs including montmorillonite, sepiolite, and palygorskite.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2015]] Comparison of two different rock types with varying mineralogical compositions.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Kaolinite can cause pore filling, while illite leads to pore bridging, both reducing permeability. ([[2015-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2015, p. 39]])


- Kaolinite is the dominant clay mineral throughout the formation, often acting as a pore lining. ([[2016-abbas|Abbas, 2016, p. 106]])

- Rectorite (illite-smectite) is a common mixed layer in shale formations. ([[2017-al-arfaj|Al-Arfaj, 2017, p. 66]])

- Clay content was used as a proxy for mineralogy in the constant-cement model. ([[2021-suleymanov|Suleymanov, 2021, p. 59]])

- Smectite (montmorillonite) has high CEC (80-150 m2/g) and is highly sensitive to water. ([[2022-alshalan|Alshalan, 2022, p. 29]])

- Smectite group (2:1 type clays) are considered most prone to swelling, with Montmorillonite being the primary member. ([[2021-khan|KHAN, 2021, p. 36]])

- OMMT is the most popular commercial rheology additive used in OBDF due to its hydrophobicity and large basal spacing. ([[2024-mahmoud|Mahmoud, 2024, p. 30]])
